description:
Rescoped from "On-demand any-key spec registration".

After .29.12.6 dropped any-keys for fns with fully-typed indirect dispatch, the only remaining cause of a kept any-key on a non-entry-point fn is the fn-as-value pattern: `apply2(double, 21)` lowers to `MakeClosure(double, [])`, which the typer registers as `(double, [any])` = double's any-key (because args are unknown at MakeClosure time). The CallClosure inside apply2 then dispatches through that any-key.

This epic eliminates the last cohort of dead any-keys via callsite-sensitive specialization for closure-valued parameters.

## Investigation findings (up-front)

- **Synthesis site**: `src/ir_lower.rs:580` — bare top-level fn name → `Prim::MakeClosure(fn_id, vec![])`. Only synthesis point for fn-as-value.
- **Any-key registration site**: `src/ir_typer.rs:422-437` — the queried-set walk registers `(lam_fn_id, [captures..., any...])` for every MakeClosure. For `MakeClosure(double, [])`, that's `(double, [any])` = double's any-key.
- **Descr surface**: `types.rs` has `ArrowSig` (function shape) but NO FnId-identity concept. Carrying FnId info through specialization needs a side-channel `HashMap<Var, FnId>` per-spec, NOT a new Descr variant. This keeps the type lattice unchanged.
- **Codegen impact**: `Term::CallClosure` dispatches through the closure header's stub_fp. To bypass: rewrite IR before codegen, lowering known-target `CallClosure → Call` (and `TailCallClosure → TailCall`). All three execution paths (interp/JIT/AOT) consume the same lowered IR — single rewrite pass covers all three.
- **Three-path parity**: trivially preserved — the rewrite happens before path selection.

## Children (DAG, atomic units)

- `.29.10.1` — Typer tracks `fn_constants: HashMap<Var, FnId>` per spec. Populated from `Let(v, MakeClosure(F, []))`. Propagated at direct `Term::Call` / `Term::TailCall`: arg `a` with `fn_constants[a] = F` contributes `fn_constants[param] = F` in the callee's per-spec FnTypes. (No spec-key change — fn_constants is side-channel.) Tests: pin propagation through 1-hop, 2-hop chains.
- `.29.10.2` — Typer's queried-set walk: at `Term::CallClosure(v, args, cont)` where `fn_constants[v] = F`, register `(F, typed_args)` as a direct-style spec query (not `(F, [any...])`); do NOT also register `(F, [any...])` from the originating MakeClosure when the closure value flows only into resolvable CallClosure sites. Tests: pin that `apply2(double, …)` registers double's narrow spec but not double's any-key.
- `.29.10.3` — IR rewrite pass (new module or `ir_rewrite` helper) runs after typer, before codegen. Walks every spec body; rewrites `Term::CallClosure(v, args, cont)` → `Term::Call(F, args, cont)` and `Term::TailCallClosure(v, args)` → `Term::TailCall(F, args)` when fn_constants[v] = F. Updates the `higher_order_callee_keeps_any_key_for_fn_as_value` test → asserts the any-key is now dropped. Regression: all existing tests stay green.

## Out of scope

- Multi-target fn-as-value (`if cond, do: f, else: g` flowed into apply2): needs finite FnId disjunction. Separate ticket.
- Closure-with-captures specialization on capture values: separate concern; current scope only handles zero-capture fn-as-value.
- Cross-module fn-as-value: typer can't see remote MakeClosure synthesis; deferred until cross-module specialization story.

## Tests (full arc)

- `apply2(double, 21)` — double's any-key NOT registered; double has a narrow `[int_lit(21)]` spec; apply2 has a specialized body.
- Closure with non-empty captures: unchanged behavior (no FnId singleton).
- Fn-value passed through Let chain: propagates.
- Fn-value passed through multiple receiver fns: propagates transitively.
- Three-path parity: existing `fact_5`, `count_100k`, closure-and-spawn fixtures stay green.

comments.text:
Investigation update: .29.10 is NOT trivial-given-.29.11. The cont-dispatch trampoline hardcodes any-key SpecIds (cont.fn_id.0), and compiling any-key bodies under all-any types makes calls that need their callees' any-keys too — the transitive closure of 'needs any-key' walks every reachable fn through indirect-dispatch chains.

The clean fix is typed indirect dispatch (.29.12): closures, spawn, receive, and continuations dispatch through typed stubs / cont-frame SpecIds rather than uniform any-key. Once that lands, .29.10 becomes truly trivial.

Removed dependency from .31.5 — that ticket now handles the any-key concern by simply skipping any-key entries in validation (any-key is typer-internal, not user-declared).

title: Lambda flow analysis for fn-as-value patterns
